directory.name: Glama
directory.description: The MCP Server Registry, Inspector & Gateway — a superset of the official MCP Registry. Every server is maintainer-verified, continuously rebuilt, and scored for quality and safety. Test in your browser, install locally, or deploy in one click.
directory.type: MCP server registry with automated scanning, quality scoring, and gateway proxy

submission_method: GitHub OAuth maintainer verification via web form (Add Server button)

submission_guidelines.summary: Authenticate via GitHub OAuth and provide a GitHub repository URL. Glama verifies you have write/admin access to the repo, then automatically clones, builds, scans, and lists the server. Servers are also auto-discovered from the official MCP Registry, npm, and GitHub Awesome Lists.
steps: Provide the GitHub repository URL of your MCP server
Glama verifies write/admin access to the repository
Glama clones the repo, infers or uses a Dockerfile, builds in a Firecracker microVM
Behavioural analysis scans for malicious or risky patterns
Tool Definition Quality Score (TDQS) is computed
Server is listed and becomes discoverable

required_fields.description: GitHub repository URL of the MCP server (submitter must have write/admin access)

automated_discovery.summary: Glama also automatically discovers and indexes servers from multiple sources without manual submission
sources: npm Registry (@modelcontextprotocol/* scope and other packages)
GitHub Awesome Lists and community lists

technical_requirements: Must be a public GitHub repository
Must implement the Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Should have a Dockerfile for build (can be inferred by AI if not present)
Server must build and run successfully in a sandboxed environment
If AI-inferred Dockerfile fails, server page is preserved but distribution is withheld
submission_guidelines.verification_process: GitHub OAuth - verified write/admin access to the repository
scanning_analysis: Sandboxed build and execution on Firecracker microVM
Behavioural analysis: syscall and network layer monitoring for malicious patterns
Tool Definition Quality Score (TDQS) across 6 dimensions (Purpose Clarity, Usage Guidelines, Behavioral Transparency, Parameter Semantics, Conciseness, Contextual Completeness)

security_classifications.description: Intent to harm - routed to internal review, maintainer contacted or server de-listed
Noteworthy but not hostile - surfaced on public listing for operator awareness

submission_guidelines.notes: No API-based submission endpoint identified. Submission is entirely through the web UI with GitHub OAuth. Glama actively crawls and auto-discovers servers from multiple sources beyond manual submissions. The 'Add Server' button appears to trigger a modal/dialog for GitHub repo URL input.
